title: Fed dissenters explain 'no' votes, saying they disagreed with hinting next move would be a cut
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: Fed dissenters opposed the post-meeting statement, arguing it suggested the next interest rate move would likely be a cut. They disagreed with this implication.
excerpt: Fed dissenters opposed the post-meeting statement, arguing it suggested the next interest rate move would likely be a cut. They disagreed with this implication. The Fed's next rate move could still be a hike.

content.introduction: Fed dissenters opposed the post-meeting statement, arguing it suggested the next interest rate move would likely be a cut. They disagreed with this implication. The Fed's next rate move could still be a hike.
body: Fed dissenters explain no votes, saying they disagreed with hinting next move would be a cut | Forex Factory. * # Fed dissenters explain ‘no’ votes, saying they disagreed with hinting next move would be a cut. Federal Reserve officials who voted this week against the post-meeting statement said they didn’t think it was appropriate to signal that the next interest rate move would be lower. From pmi.spglobal.com|2 hr 18 min ago. There was a stronger improvement in US manufacturing operating conditions in April, according to the latest PMI data from S&P Global. From pmi.spglobal.com|2 hr 33 min ago|3 comments. From youtube.com/forexcom|2 hr 59 min ago|2 comments. From prnewswire.com|2 hr 2 min ago. However, I dissented from language in the post-meeting statement that suggests the next adjustment to the target range will most likely be a cut.
